title: Ontario Commits $500 Million to Boost Local Critical Minerals Processing and Strengthen Supply Chain
content: Ontario is investing $500 million to create a Critical Minerals Processing Fund to boost the provinceâ€™s capacity to process critical minerals locally. This fund supports expanding existing facilities and building new ones to ensure minerals mined in Ontario are refined within the province, protecting jobs and fostering a resilient, competitive supply chain. The initiative aligns with Ontarioâ€™s 2025 Budget goals to strengthen the economy, reduce reliance on external processing amid global market uncertainties, and secure high-value manufacturing jobs, particularly in key sectors like automotive. The investment positions Ontario as a global leader in critical minerals by promoting a made-in-Ontario supply chain that adds value locally and supports long-term economic growth.
author: Analysts Account
